# x2sys_solve

```julia
x2sys_solve(cmd0::String="", arg1=nothing; kwargs...)
```

Solve for systematic corrections from crossovers.

(**Warning: Manual translate by Claude. Needs revision**)

Description
-----------

**x2sys_solve** will use the supplied crossover information to solve
for systematic corrections that can then be applied per track to improve
data quality. Seven different systematic corrections can be solved for using a
least-squares approach. **Note**: Only one data column can be processed at
a time.

Required Arguments
------------------

- *COE_list* : -- Name of file with the required crossover columns as produced by
   {{< gmtref x2sys_list >}}. Can be a file name or GMTdataset. If **binary_in** is used
   then the first two columns are expected to hold the integer track IDs; otherwise
   we expect the trailing text to hold the text string names of the two tracks.

- **C** or **col** or **column** : -- *column="name"*\
   Specify which data column you want to process. Needed for proper
   formatting of the output correction table and must match the same
   option used in {{< gmtref x2sys_list >}} when preparing the input data.

- **T** or **TAG** or **tag** : -- *tag="tagname"*\
   Specify the system tag for this compilation.

- **E** or **model** or **correction** : -- *correction=type*\
   The correction type you wish to model. Choose among the following
   functions f(**p**), where **p** are the *m* parameters per track that we
   will fit simultaneously using a least squares approach. Each type implies
   a certain input data record format:

   - **:c** or **:constant** or **:offset** - fit f(**p**) = *a* (a constant offset);
     records must contain COE, ID1, ID2.
   - **:d** or **:drift** or **:distance** - fit f(**p**) = *a* + *b* \* *d* (linear drift);
     *d* is distance along track; records must contain d1, d2, COE, ID1, ID2.
   - **:g** or **:gravity** - fit f(**p**) = *a* + *b* sin(y)² (1980-1930 gravity correction);
     records must contain crossing latitude y, COE, ID1, ID2.
   - **:h** or **:heading** - fit f(**p**) = *a* + *b* cos(h) + *c* cos(2h) + *d* sin(h) + *e* sin(2h)
     (magnetic heading correction); *h* is heading at crossover; records must contain h1, h2, COE, ID1, ID2.
   - **:s** or **:scale** - fit f(**p**) = *a* \* z (a unit scale correction);
     *z* is the data value at the crossover; records must contain z1, z2, ID1, ID2.
   - **:t** or **:time** - fit f(**p**) = *a* + *b* \* (*t - t0*) (linear drift with time);
     *t - t0* is the time along the track since start; records must contain t1-t0, t2-t0, COE, ID1, ID2.
   - **:z** or **:full** - fit f(**p**) = *a* + *b* \* z (offset plus unit scale correction);
     *z* is the data value at the crossover; records must contain z1, z2, ID1, ID2.

Optional Arguments
------------------

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_V.qmd >}}

- **W** or **weights** : -- *weights=true* **|** *weights=:unweighted*\
   Means that each input record has an extra column just before the ID columns
   with the composite weight for each crossover record. These are used to obtain a
   weighted least squares solution [no weights]. Use **:unweighted** (or **:u**)
   to report unweighted mean/std [Default reports weighted stats].

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_bi.qmd >}}

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_di.qmd >}}

Notes
-----

Most of the model corrections involve a constant offset. Because crossovers are
differences between values, any absolute level will cancel out and hence the
constant offsets we obtain are relative to an undetermined absolute level. To
obtain a solvable solution we add the constraint that the sum of all constant
offsets equal zero. If the tracks form clusters in which no tracks from one
cluster cross any track from another cluster then these are two independent
data sets and require their own constraint equation for their offsets. We
determine the number of clusters and automatically add the required constraint
equations. If you need a particular reference track to have a particular offset
(e.g., 0) then you can subtract the offset you found from every track correction
and add in the desired offset.

Examples
--------

To fit a simple bias offset to faa for all tracks under the MGD77 tag:

```julia
using GMT

# Step 1: Extract crossover data
D = x2sys_list("COE_data.txt", tag="MGD77", column="faa", output="nc")
write("faa_coe.txt", D)

# Step 2: Solve for constant offset
corrections = x2sys_solve("faa_coe.txt", tag="MGD77", column="faa", correction=:constant)
```

To fit a faa linear drift with time instead:

```julia
using GMT

# Step 1: Extract crossover data with time
D = x2sys_list("COE_data.txt", tag="MGD77", column="faa", output="nTc")
write("faa_coe.txt", D)

# Step 2: Solve for time drift
corrections = x2sys_solve("faa_coe.txt", tag="MGD77", column="faa", correction=:time)
```

To estimate heading corrections based on magnetic crossovers:

```julia
using GMT

# Step 1: Extract crossover data with headings
D = x2sys_list("COE_data.txt", tag="MGD77", column="mag", output="nhc")
write("mag_coe.txt", D)

# Step 2: Solve for heading correction
corrections = x2sys_solve("mag_coe.txt", tag="MGD77", column="mag", correction=:heading)
```

To estimate unit scale corrections based on bathymetry crossovers:

```julia
using GMT

# Step 1: Extract crossover data with z values
D = x2sys_list("COE_data.txt", tag="MGD77", column="depth", output="nz")
write("depth_coe.txt", D)

# Step 2: Solve for scale correction
corrections = x2sys_solve("depth_coe.txt", tag="MGD77", column="depth", correction=:scale)
```

To solve with weighted least squares:

```julia
using GMT
corrections = x2sys_solve("weighted_coe.txt", tag="MGD77", column="faa",
                          correction=:constant, weights=true)
```

See Also
--------

- {{< gmtref x2sys_init >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_binlist >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_cross >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_datalist >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_get >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_list >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_put >}}, {{< gmtref x2sys_report >}}
- [Alphabetical Function List](../alphabetical.html)

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
using GMT
GMT.doc_source_links("x2sys_solve")
```
